findings.evidence: New theorem starts at `code/TauCeti/MeasureTheory/Measure/Prokhorov.lean:86`; existing APIs are `mathlib/Mathlib/MeasureTheory/Measure/Prokhorov.lean:211: lemma isCompact_setOf_finiteMeasure_mass_le_compl_isCompact_le` and `mathlib/Mathlib/MeasureTheory/Measure/FiniteMeasure.lean:727: theorem tendsto_iff_forall_integral_tendsto`.
findings.fix: Refactor this theorem around Mathlib's finite-measure compactness theorem, then use the finite-measure bounded-continuous integral characterization; keep only the extra real-line support/tail wrapper needed by TauCeti.
findings.issue: `finite_measure_subseq_limit` rebuilds finite-measure Prokhorov compactness and weak integral convergence by normalizing to probability measures, creating a parallel API for a result Mathlib already packages for `FiniteMeasure`.

summary: The completely-monotone Bernstein helpers appear non-duplicative, but the new finite-measure subsequence extraction overlaps substantially with existing Mathlib Prokhorov and finite-measure weak-convergence APIs.
